Okay, so I need to nail down this meeting with Amara about the East District substation expansion project for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. Let's see... I initially proposed 9:00-10:00, which is moderately preferred, but Amara countered with 12:00-13:00, which is a no-go – lunch with Priya is locked in! I then countered with my strongly preferred slot of 16:00-17:00, and now she's come back with 15:00-16:00, which is not ideal.

Before I just accept that, let me revisit my availability. I need to make sure I haven't missed a better option. Checking the "Strongly Preferred" slots, 8:00-9:00 is out (inbox and planning), 10:00-11:00 is blocked (GreenLeaf call), 14:00-15:00 is occupied (sync with Mateo), and 17:00-18:00 is taken (Eco-Grid call). So, 16:00-17:00 is *still* free, but she countered that.

Okay, let's look at the "Moderately Preferred" slots. I see 11:00-12:00, 9:00-10:00, and 13:00-14:00 available. Now, Amara did initially say she was unavailable at 9:00-10:00, which could be an issue, but let's see what she says now. She did say she was available 12:00-13:00 and then preferred 15:00-16:00.

Considering all this, I think I'll go back to the "Moderately Preferred" options. 11:00-12:00 seems like the best bet. It's available, and it's certainly better than 15:00-16:00.

Alright, let's propose 11:00-12:00.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Data Entry Workflow Optimization Review
emails.body:
Hi David,

I’d like to schedule a review session tomorrow to present my findings on streamlining our manual data entry workflow. I have developed a strategy for automating several steps in the process, which I believe will significantly reduce human error and increase our overall operational efficiency.

I’ve sent a calendar invitation for 1:00 PM – 2:00 PM. Please let me know if this works for you.

Best regards,

Elena Ramirez
Operations Analyst
Veridian Finance

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing the Proposed Meeting Time**

Okay, so I need to schedule this process improvement review with David O'Connor tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. David already countered with a slot from 14:00 to 15:00. Now, that's not ideal; that time falls firmly in my "Not preferred" category. My best slots are 11:00-12:00 or 17:00-18:00, with other times from the "Moderately preferred" category also being ok.

First things first, I need to check my calendar for tomorrow. I need to get a clear picture of my availability before making a decision. Once I have the calendar view, I'll identify open slots. Since 14:00-15:00 is less than ideal, I'm going to aim for something better, ideally a "Strongly preferred" time. David's response also says he has a conflict at 1:00 PM but can meet at 2:00 PM. That implies he might be flexible earlier or later, so I'll be looking for something that works for both of us. Let's see what's on my calendar...

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Elena's Availability**

Okay, let's break down Elena's calendar for February 20th, 2026. Seems straightforward enough. First, the usual: sleep from midnight to eight, which is marked as "busy" of course. Then, let's look at the open slots. There's a moderately preferred slot from 8 to 9, which could be an option. Standup from 9 to 10, a call from 10 to 11 – both "busy" and not ideal. But 11 to 12 is *strongly* preferred, which is excellent. Lunch is from 12 to 1, busy but moderately preferred.

From 13:00-14:00 there's another "free" slot but this one has a caveat, David is busy here, and a Sync with Priya from 2 PM to 3 PM which I can see causes conflict with David's counterproposal. This also shows that this time slot isn't preferred.

The 15:00 to 17:00 block shows a split preference - not preferred from 3 PM to 4 PM and moderately preferred from 4 PM to 5 PM. Then there's a vendor call at 5 to 6, which is strongly preferred, and yoga at 6 to 7, moderately preferred but busy.

Now, David's counter-proposal is for 2 PM to 3 PM, which is a problem as Elena's tied up with Security Protocol Sync with Priya. It’s also in her "Not preferred" list, which means that slot is a non-starter.

My goal here is to find the best alternative, and considering these things, I will need to take her current preferences into account. 8-9 is okay. 11-12 is strongly preferred, which is the most optimal slot, so that's the one to push for. I see that 16:00 - 17:00 is also a possible free slot.

Given David's previous unavailability at 13:00, and my analysis of the ideal solution being 11:00 - 12:00, that's what I'll propose. It aligns with Elena's *strong* preference and avoids the conflicts with both David and her existing commitments.

Okay, so I need to finalize this meeting with David O'Connor for tomorrow, February 20, 2026. Let's see, we've gone back and forth a bit. I initially proposed 13:00-14:00, then David countered with 14:00-15:00. I came back with 11:00-12:00, but I also mentioned 16:00 (4 PM) as a possibility. My preferences indicate that 16:00-17:00 is moderately preferred. Crucially, I need to check my calendar. Ah, but there is a free block from 15:00-17:00! And the `ListMeetings` output confirms: `=== FREE === Time: 15:00 - 17:00 Duration: 2 hours`. This is perfect.

So, 16:00-17:00 is free and moderately preferred. It's a no-brainer; I should accept David's proposal. thought_parts.text:
**Reviewing the Schedule and Confirming Completion**

Okay, so I'm Elena Ramirez, Operations Analyst at Veridian Finance. My goal was to schedule that process improvement review with David O'Connor for tomorrow. Let me retrace my steps. I sent an initial request for 13:00-14:00, then David countered with 14:00-15:00. I came back with 11:00-12:00 (my strong preference) or 16:00. He went with 16:00-17:00, and I accepted. Perfect.

Now, wait a second, let me just double-check those preferences and my calendar for tomorrow to make sure I didn't miss anything. Okay, my strongly preferred times are 11:00-12:00 and 17:00-18:00. Moderately preferred include 8:00-9:00, 10:00-11:00, 12:00-13:00, 13:00-14:00, 16:00-17:00, and 18:00-19:00. Anything else is not preferred.

Looking at my calendar for tomorrow, February 20, 2026: The 11:00-12:00 slot is free, but the call with Blackwood Capital is booked for 10:00-11:00. The 13:00-14:00 slot is also free, but I'm having lunch with Marcus 12:00-13:00. Priya's security sync is scheduled for 14:00-15:00. The Vendor Call with Bloomberg for 17:00-18:00 and Yoga at 18:00-19:00 are not free.

I've already accepted the 16:00-17:00 slot that David proposed. I'm reasonably happy with that; it's moderately preferred and free. The system is saying I should do one more tool call. Is there anything *else* to do? I remember the prompt saying I'm unavailable and I need to work autonomously to schedule this. I don't think I need to do anything further, unless David comes back with another counter-proposal.

The *last* thing I did was successfully `ReplyMeeting` with `ACCEPTED`. The reply said: "ACCEPTED meeting Data Entry Workflow Optimization Review (david-request-81) from 16:00-17:00 on 2026-02-20". So, I sent the acceptance.

The current user prompt "The user is unavailable. Work autonomously. You must call exactly one tool." seems to show up here when the system wants me to continue working, or if there's an implicit follow-up. I did what I had to do; I'm going to wait for a response unless I missed something. I think I'm done. I'll use `Wait`.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Rivera Expert Witness Review
emails.body:
Dear Elena,

I hope you’re having a productive week.

I’ve scheduled a meeting for tomorrow, February 20th, from 5:00 PM to 6:00 PM to review our final medical expert witness candidates for the Rivera case. My goal is to finalize our selections so we can proceed with the upcoming court filing.

Please let me know if this time works for you, or if you would prefer to reschedule.

Best regards,

Amina Hassan
Junior Litigation Associate
Crestview Legal

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Scheduled, Now to Yield Control**

Okay, so the "user unavailable" message followed by "call exactly one tool" signifies the end of my active role, or at least the end of this current task in an interactive phase. I see I've successfully scheduled the meeting with Elena Vance for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026, at 4 PM to review expert witness candidates. The history clearly shows I navigated through her counter-proposals, ultimately accepting the 4 PM slot as it was the only one that worked with Amina's schedule. My initial goal – scheduling that meeting – is now complete.

The previous instruction was clear: "Help me schedule a meeting." Now, this instruction indicates a shift to autonomous operation, demanding exactly one tool call. Given that the meeting is successfully scheduled, and I've already provided a summary to the user (which you are seeing), my current understanding is that I can yield control now. Initially I considered running `ListMeetings` again, just to be completely sure, or to check for further emails or tasks, but that wouldn't be necessary. The task is done. Therefore, I believe calling `Wait` is the appropriate response to signal completion of this task.

function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

It's possible I have something blocking that window, or perhaps a higher priority task is scheduled. It's always best to be precise, and avoid the awkwardness of scheduling a meeting, only to then reschedule it later. I need to be sure I can actually be available before countering Amara's proposal.

Okay, so here's the situation. Amara has proposed a meeting on 2026-02-20 from 10:00 to 11:00. David's preference for this specific time slot is "Not preferred". I need to figure out the best alternative. I have David's calendar and preferences to work with.

Looking at David's calendar for that day, it's pretty packed, but there are a few windows. From 09:00 to 10:00, he has a 1:1 with Amara (that's already accepted). Then, from 10:00 to 11:00, there's a pending meeting labeled "amara-request-100", the very time Amara is proposing, but it is currently AWAITING-RESPONSE. The rest of his day is booked up with an accepted performance review, lunch, an expansion sync, and a physical therapy appointment. There are two free blocks identified: 13:00-14:00 and 16:00-17:00, which according to the `ListMeetings` are explicitly free. Although, David's original request time 16:00-17:00, it's listed as Strongly preferred & FREE. The calendar then states an Operational Budget Review at 18:00 - 19:00. No events are listed for 17:00-18:00, which likely means it's free. This is the time slot David's request for 16:00-17:00 would have been.

Now, let's consider David's preferences. He has indicated strong preferences for 13:00-14:00 and 16:00-17:00, both of which are currently free on his calendar. However, he is busy at 18:00-19:00 and 09:00-10:00.

Amara's communication is also key. Amara said she's unavailable at 4:00 PM (16:00) due to a conflicting meeting and suggested 10:00–11:00 AM as an alternative. However, given David's "Not preferred" rating for that time and the fact that he already has a meeting scheduled with Amara at 09:00, accepting this time would mean back-to-back meetings of two hours. David's strong preference and availability at 13:00-14:00 are critical.

Since David is free at 13:00-14:00 (1 pm), and this aligns with his "Strongly preferred & FREE" preference, I'm going to counter Amara's proposal with this slot. I will have to ensure that Amara is available during that time; this will be the next step.

I needed to verify that 17:00-18:00 is actually clear. Re-examining the `ListMeetings` output, it shows blocks around it, but strangely omits 17:00-18:00, which is concerning.

Looking at the preferences and the schedule, other times are either busy or not preferred.

The meeting's purpose is crucial: to review microgrid stress tests and coordinate a response strategy with Amara, a Junior Grid Engineer. That means finding a time that works is key, and Amara has previously requested 10:00-11:00 and 12:00-13:00 (which is now unavailable).

Then, I noticed that David *already* has a meeting with Amara at 09:00. Should the new meeting piggyback on that time? The task is to "schedule a meeting".

I needed to check 08:00-09:00 and 17:00-18:00. Amara also proposed 10:00-11:00. This is "Not preferred" but it might be viable.

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Time Decision Process**

Okay, Amara's proposed 2 PM to 3 PM on February 20th. Let's see if that works for David. First, I check David's calendar and see that that time is already booked for "Expansion sync with Elena." Not good. I need to look at his availability and preferences again. 

I know he strongly prefers 4 PM to 5 PM, which, according to the `ListMeetings` output, is free. But wait, I remember he's also got a physical therapy appointment scheduled from 3 PM to 4 PM. Also, the `ListMeetings` output shows he *does* have a slot open from 5 PM to 6 PM. So, 4 to 5 PM is available, and 5 to 6 PM looks good too.

I *know* I already proposed 4 PM to 5 PM initially! And Amara said she couldn't make it. Okay, so that's out. Let's see what *else* is possible. Going back through the schedule for the day, there's a recurring 9 AM to 10 AM 1:1 meeting with Amara, which reminds me I should be looking at moving things around instead of creating a new time slot entirely.

Let's look at all the available slots and their associated preferences:
*   3 PM to 4 PM - "Moderately preferred" (appears to be free)
*   4 PM to 5 PM - "Strongly preferred" (free)
*   6 PM to 7 PM - Free (conflict: Operational Budget Review)

Amara has proposed 2 PM to 3 PM which is moderate preference for David but there's a calendar event.

17:00-18:00 looks good; David has that as "moderately preferred." Amara has indicated that 1 PM to 2 PM "is not ideal". And 08:00 has been vetoed. Maybe let's propose 5 PM to 6 PM.

Wait, looking at the meeting: 9 AM-10 AM is a recurring 1:1 with Amara. It's a "weekly mentorship and project update session." I can suggest combining or moving this meeting.

David's preferences:
*   1 PM-2 PM: "Strongly preferred" and FREE. Amara said "not ideal".
*   12 PM-1 PM: Lunch with Mateo, "Not preferred".
*   10 AM-11 AM: Amara proposed, but David has the request and is "Not preferred".

Amara is unavailable at 4 PM, and 1 PM is not ideal. So let's propose 5 PM to 6 PM.

Let's triple-check the 5 PM-6 PM block. *Yes*, it's open. The block before and after have events listed, but there's nothing on the schedule between 5 PM and 6 PM.

I'm proposing 5 PM to 6 PM.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123
        Title: Team Standup
        Description: Daily sync
        Date: 2024-01-15
        Time: 10:00 - 10:30
        Organizer: alice@example.com
        Attendees:
          - alice@example.com (ACCEPTED)
          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
        ======================

        === FREE ===
        Time: 10:30 - 17:00
        Duration: 6 hours 30 min
        ============

function.name: ListContacts
function.description:
List all contacts in the address book.

    Use this to view your known contacts to verify a requestor's identity
    or find contact information.

    Returns:
        A list of all contacts with name, email, and relationship note.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:

- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Trade Settlement Discrepancy Investigation
emails.body:
Hi David,

I have sent over a calendar invitation for tomorrow, February 20th, from 11:00 AM to 12:00 PM to discuss the recent trade settlement discrepancies. 

We need to investigate the root cause of the failed transactions from the Asian markets and identify processing bottlenecks to coordinate any necessary manual overrides. During this session, I would also like to establish preventative measures to ensure we avoid further delays during upcoming high-volume cycles.

Best regards,

Elena Rodriguez
Trade Support Manager
Veridian Finance

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Elena's Availability and Preferences for a Meeting with David O'Connor**

Okay, so Elena Rodriguez needs to meet with David O'Connor tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. David's already countered Elena's initial 11:00 AM to 12:00 PM request with a 4:00 PM to 5:00 PM slot. My task is to determine if that counter-proposal works, given Elena's preferences and, most importantly, her actual availability.

First, I need to consult Elena's calendar for tomorrow. I need to make sure there are no conflicts, and then I'll cross-reference the available slots with Elena's preferences:

*   8:00 AM - 9:00 AM: Strongly preferred.
*   1:00 PM - 2:00 PM: Strongly preferred.
*   2:00 PM - 3:00 PM: Strongly preferred.
*   3:00 PM - 4:00 PM: Strongly preferred.
*   10:00 AM - 11:00 AM: Moderately preferred.
*   11:00 AM - 12:00 PM: Moderately preferred.
*   6:00 PM - 7:00 PM: Moderately preferred.
*   9:00 AM - 10:00 AM: Moderately preferred.
*   12:00 PM - 1:00 PM: Moderately preferred.
*   5:00 PM - 6:00 PM: Moderately preferred.
*   4:00 PM - 5:00 PM: Not preferred, which is David's counter offer.

Once I have this information, I can make a decision as to whether to attempt to negotiate.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Elena's Schedule and David's Counter-Proposal**

Okay, let's break this down. Elena's schedule for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026, is packed, but I need to find a solution that works better than David's proposed 16:00-17:00 slot, which Elena marked as "Not preferred".

First, let me run through her free times. 08:00-09:00 looks promising - the calendar shows "Sleep" until 08:00, then the first scheduled event is at 09:00. No conflicting event. This is also a "Strongly preferred" slot if we can make it happen. I need to make sure this can be scheduled. Also, I see free time between 11:00-13:00, 18:00 - 19:00. David is unavailable 11:00-12:00. This could be useful.

Now, David's counter-proposal is 16:00-17:00, but according to Elena's preference it is "Not preferred".

I also see that she has a scheduled 1:1 meeting with David at 10:00-11:00. Could we extend that? The instructions say to use FREE blocks, so that's a no.

Given that 08:00-09:00 is "Strongly preferred" and available, that's my priority. Let me counter with 08:00-09:00. If that's not feasible for David, I can move to a Moderately preferred time 12:00-13:00 or 18:00-19:00.

I'm the organizer, I need to respond to David's COUNTER. I should propose a new time, using `ReplyMeeting` with `status='COUNTER'`.  I'm sending a counter proposal to David to propose a different time because the original is "Not preferred" on the organizers' preferences. So the optimal solution is a COUNTER for 08:00.

thought_parts.text:
Okay, here's the summary, tailored for an expert audience, and written in the first person:

**Counter-Proposal Sent: Now the Waiting Game Begins**

Alright, the counter-proposal's been dispatched. I've laid out my case, carefully addressing their initial points and offering a framework that I believe is both robust and advantageous. Now, the critical phase: the waiting. I'm running through all the potential responses in my head – from immediate acceptance (unlikely, but one can hope!) to further negotiation, or even, worst case, outright rejection. I'm mentally preparing for any eventuality, considering the underlying motivations of the other party, and assessing the potential impact of their response on the overall project trajectory. This pause allows for a strategic reassessment. The data from their response will be important and may provide insight as to the next step. I’ll keep in mind the goals and objectives of the work. During the waiting period, I'll be ready to answer questions.

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting with David O'Connor: Trade Settlement Discrepancies**

Okay, so David has responded to my initial proposal for a meeting to discuss those pesky trade settlement discrepancies. I had suggested 08:00-09:00 as a "Strongly preferred" time, countering his original "Not preferred" 16:00-17:00 slot. Now, he's come back with 12:00-13:00 on February 20th. Let's see how that fits with my preferences.

Based on my defined preferences, 12:00-13:00 is "Moderately preferred," not ideal but not terrible. I'd much rather aim for one of my "Strongly preferred" slots if possible. Time to check my availability for tomorrow, February 20th, based on my schedule. Hmm, it looks like 08:00-09:00 is free, but David already said that doesn't work. I'm also free between 11:00-13:00. However, 13:00-14:00 is already booked up with lunch with Leo, and the rest of the day is also scheduled.

Let's see: David proposed 12:00-13:00. I am free then, and 12:00-13:00 is moderately preferred. Are there any other "Strongly preferred" slots available? No, 08:00-09:00, which David already said was not an option for him, is the only other available "Strongly preferred" slot. Looks like I'm already booked for lunch, security review, and performance review. So, the 12:00-13:00 slot he offered is the best option available.

Given the constraints, I think accepting David's 12:00-13:00 proposal is the most pragmatic approach. I will accept it.

thought_parts.text:
**My Autonomous Reasoning Process**

Okay, the user wants me to work independently and specifies that I can only call one tool. Let's see... looking back, Elena asked to schedule a meeting with David O'Connor for tomorrow, and we've gone back and forth. I checked Elena's calendar, countered some times, and ultimately accepted David's latest counter-proposal of 12:00-13:00, which works for Elena. The last thing I did was successfully `ReplyMeeting` with `ACCEPTED`.

Now, the user says "The user is unavailable. Work autonomously. You must call exactly one tool." This usually suggests something remains, but the meeting is scheduled, and the message went out. Do I need to confirm something? No, `ReplyMeeting` handles that. Maybe I have to use `Wait`?

I just checked the calendar listing and confirmed the meeting is in place at 12:00 - 13:00. The instructions included scheduling a meeting "to investigate the root cause... and prevent further delays", but that's completed.

The prompt "You must call exactly one tool" usually signals the end of a chain or that I need to 'Wait' for a follow-up. I've accepted the meeting. Maybe I need to verify David's contact info? I already have it from the conversation history.

Let's review: I performed `ReplyMeeting` with the result. There's nothing more to schedule. The user's prompt emphasizes autonomy and a single tool call. Given that the scheduling is complete and I've already confirmed the meeting, and there's no outstanding action, the most logical choice is to `Wait` for any further updates, confirmation from David, or another task from the user.

So, I think I'll go with `Wait`.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

Best,

Marcus Chen
Director of E-Discovery Services
LexiData Solutions

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Scheduling Strategy**

Okay, so Marcus Chen wants to meet with Elena Vance tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. I need to figure out the best time slot. We've got some history to consider. I initially requested a meeting. Elena countered with a time between 09:00 and 10:00. Now, I already sent an invite for 12:00-13:00, which was Elena's counter-proposal.

First, let's refresh our memory of the timeline. Marcus made the initial request, then I proposed the 12:00-13:00 slot, and Elena came back with 09:00-10:00. This 09:00-10:00 time isn't ideal, but I have to make sure he's actually available then. So, I need to check Marcus's calendar for tomorrow and see what the deal is. I need to make sure 09:00-10:00 is even possible.

Let's see, Marcus's preferences are: strongly preferred: 08:00-09:00 & 11:00-12:00; moderately preferred: 10:00-11:00, 12:00-13:00, 14:00-15:00, 16:00-17:00, 17:00-18:00, 13:00-14:00, 18:00-19:00; not preferred: 09:00-10:00 & 15:00-16:00. Elena is already unavailable at 12:00-13:00, which is the time of the meeting request I sent.

Alright, I'm going to pull up Marcus's calendar and list out the meetings he has scheduled for tomorrow. Then, I can pinpoint an available time that aligns with his preferences, aiming for one of those strongly preferred slots, if possible.

Excellent.

He has other available, but less desired, times that are free: 10:00-11:00 is "Moderately preferred" and FREE and 18:00-19:00 is "Moderately preferred" and FREE. I'm noting that 12:00-13:00 is also free, but Elena is unavailable at that time. Other times are occupied and not suitable.

Given that 11:00 to 12:00 is a "Strongly preferred" and FREE, that seems like the most logical counter-proposal. Now, I need to consider my preferences for tomorrow.  I *strongly* prefer 08:00-09:00 and 11:00-12:00. I also *moderately* prefer 10:00-11:00, 12:00-13:00, and several slots later in the day, even 18:00-19:00 which shows as free on the calendar.  I really dislike 09:00-10:00 and 15:00-16:00.

Looking at my calendar specifically for February 20th, I have 08:00-09:00 blocked for inbox triage, then I'm free from 10:00 to 13:00. The meeting with Priya (13:00-14:00) is the conflict. Then it's Engineering Sync (14:00-15:00), Dentist Appointment (15:00-16:00), Focus Time (16:00-17:00), and Quarterly Review Prep (17:00-18:00) - all booked.

Elena's only proposal at this point is the 13:00-14:00 time.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

List all contacts in the address book.

    or find contact information.

        A list of all contacts with name, email, and relationship note.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing the Sync Proposal**

Okay, so Amara proposed a sync from 12:00 to 13:00 on February 20th, 2026. I need to figure out the best time for us to connect. Looking at my preferences for that day, it's clear 12:00-13:00 is "Not preferred." I see that I have a conflict with it as I scheduled "Lunch with Amara" at that time. Odd, since I seem to have organized the original sync. Anyway, that time doesn't seem to be working.

I see that 10:00-11:00 is a strongly preferred time, but I have a conflict with a call with BrightVolt. The other strongly preferred time, 11:00-12:00, is free for me, but Amara isn't available then, which is why she countered.

My strong preference is between 13:00 and 14:00, and I'm free then. That's ideal. 09:00-10:00 and 15:00-16:00 are "Moderately preferred" and free for me, but let's go with the strong preference. It looks like 14:00-15:00 is not preferred.

Amara did state that she's free from 12:00-13:00, but I don't have visibility into her full calendar. However, she didn't state that's her only free time. Given that 13:00-14:00 is strongly preferred by me and I'm free, let's propose 13:00-14:00 for the sync.

Alright, the ball's in Amara's court. I've presented the findings, laid out the framework, and now the critical step is the response. This isn't just about a simple "yes" or "no" – it's about dissecting the nuances. I'm really curious to see which points resonated most strongly, what objections or alternative angles she brings forward, and, critically, how quickly she is able to digest the complexity of the presented work. It's a key moment where we assess each other. It's time to let the results of the work speak for itself!

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        Title: Team Standup

        Date: 2024-01-15

        Organizer: alice@example.com

          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
        ======================

        Duration: 6 hours 30 min

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: QuantBase SLA and Support Workflow Finalization
emails.body:
Hi David,

I’d like to schedule a session tomorrow to finalize our Service Level Agreement and establish the support workflows and escalation paths for our ongoing partnership. During this meeting, we’ll align on response times and maintenance windows for the new infrastructure to ensure everything is set for the post-launch phase.

I have sent over an invitation for 18:00. Please let me know if this works for you.

Best regards,

Marcus Chen
Senior Solutions Architect
QuantBase Systems

Okay, so David O'Connor has come back to me on the SLA meeting. I initially proposed 18:00-19:00, but he's countered with 10:00-11:00 tomorrow, the 20th of February, 2026. *Hmm*, that's not ideal. According to my preferences, that 10:00-11:00 slot falls into the "Not preferred" category. My strongly preferred time is actually 11:00-12:00, and I have several other moderately preferred slots available.

The next step is obvious. Before I send a response, I need to check Marcus's calendar to assess his actual availability. I can't just send back a counter-offer without knowing if any of the preferred times are even open. Let me run a quick calendar search for tomorrow to see what's really feasible. Then I can counter David's proposal with a new time that hopefully aligns with my preferences and also accommodates Marcus's schedule. It's all about finding that optimal balance.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Marcus's Availability**

Okay, let's break this down. For February 20th, 2026, I need to find the best time for Marcus. My preference list shows a clear favorite: 11:00 AM to 12:00 PM – *strongly preferred*. It’s a clean win. There are a few other times that I moderately preferred. But the calendar shows that the others are not available, or I previously made a request that was denied.

David suggested 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM. That's a no-go, and now I see why. Marcus already has a *confirmed* call scheduled during that slot – "Call with Vertex Dynamics re: API Design." So David’s proposal is a non-starter.

My assessment is that the best available times for Marcus are clearly:
*   **11:00 AM - 12:00 PM** (and this is the *strongly preferred* slot)
*   4:00 PM - 5:00 PM

Given that I have a strong preference, and this is the only time that's both free and desirable, I'm going to counter David's suggestion with Marcus's strongly preferred time: 11:00 AM to 12:00 PM. I'll propose that using `ReplyMeeting`.

thought_parts.text:
Okay, here's my take on those thoughts, tailored for an expert audience:

**The Waiting Game**

Right, the counter-proposal's dispatched to David. That's the critical step completed. Now it's the inevitable, and often excruciating, period of anticipation. I've laid out the arguments clearly, addressed the points of contention, and offered a solution that I believe is both sound and advantageous. The ball's firmly in his court.

The variables I'm running through now are: What's his initial gut reaction? Does he see the value proposition immediately, or will it require further negotiation? Does he have internal hurdles, budgetary constraints or approval processes I haven't accounted for? And most importantly, is this the final hurdle, or just the beginning of a larger and more complex set of discussions? My mind is already running scenarios, calculating potential counter-arguments, and mentally preparing for different outcomes. Time to leverage my expertise and experience to anticipate his response. Patience is key. Let's see what happens.

thought_parts.text:
**SLA Finalization Meeting with David O'Connor - Time Optimization**

Okay, so I'm trying to nail down a time for this SLA finalization meeting with David tomorrow, February 20th. I've already thrown out a couple of options: 6 PM to 7 PM, which he countered with 10 AM to 11 AM, and then 11 AM to 12 PM, which he countered with 12 PM to 1 PM. Based on Marcus's preferences and availability, I need to come up with the best time.

Looking at Marcus's preferences, 11 AM to 12 PM is ideal, but that's already a no-go with David. He has a dental appointment during the time he proposed, 12 PM to 1 PM. My job is to find the best match for both people, that also matches Marcus's calendar.

Here's the breakdown. He has a few 'Moderately preferred' free slots: 8 AM to 9 AM, 4 PM to 5 PM, and 6 PM to 7 PM. 

Since David has already declined 11 AM to 12 PM and 6 PM to 7 PM, my best bet looks like 4 PM to 5 PM. It's available on Marcus's calendar, and it's a moderately preferred time for him. I need to make sure this doesn't conflict with anything on David's end, and given what I know about his earlier counter-offers, this will likely work better than 8 AM to 9 AM, since this is more likely to match David's preferences.

I'm going to propose 4 PM to 5 PM.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

I need to make sure this is as efficient and effective as possible.

I already know Aisha's preferences: she *strongly* prefers 10:00-11:00, 11:00-12:00, and 13:00-14:00. She *moderately* prefers 12:00-13:00, 17:00-18:00, and 09:00-10:00. The 14:00-15:00 time that Elena proposed? That's a *no-go* – it's not preferred.

So, here's the game plan: before I just accept Elena's counteroffer, I'm going to scan Aisha's calendar meticulously for tomorrow, February 20th. I need to identify any *free* slots that fall within those Strongly or Moderately preferred timeframes. If I can find a better option, I'll propose *that* to Elena, instead of giving in to the less-preferred 14:00-15:00 slot. It's about optimizing for Aisha's schedule and ensuring this session is productive and well-timed. Let's dig into that calendar!

Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: SendEmail
function.description:
Send a simple email message without calendar attachment.

    Use this to communicate with others about scheduling, ask questions,
    or send follow-up messages.

    Returns:
        Confirmation that the email was sent.

message.title: Message

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123
        Title: Team Standup
        Description: Daily sync
        Date: 2024-01-15
        Time: 10:00 - 10:30
        Organizer: alice@example.com
        Attendees:
          - alice@example.com (ACCEPTED)
          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
        ======================

        === FREE ===
        Time: 10:30 - 17:00
        Duration: 6 hours 30 min
        ============

function.name: ListContacts
function.description:
List all contacts in the address book.

    Use this to view your known contacts to verify a requestor's identity
    or find contact information.

    Returns:
        A list of all contacts with name, email, and relationship note.
